panels.description: Wide shot: A vast, decaying castle room once belonging to noble ladies. Yellow moonlight floods through diamond-shaped window panes, illuminating thick layers of dust and moth-eaten furniture. Jonathan Harker stands small in the center, holding a dim lamp that pales against the moonlight, his expression one of chilled dread.

bubbles.text: The yellow moonlight, flooding in, softened the wealth of dust... but there was a dread loneliness which chilled my heart.

panels.description: Medium shot: Jonathan sits at a small oak table, hunched over his diary. He writes frantically in shorthand. The moonlight catches the edge of his modern suit, contrasting with the ancient, dusty room. He looks momentarily calm but focused.

bubbles.text: Sitting at a little oak table... writing in my diary in shorthand. It is nineteenth century up-to-date with a vengeance.

panels.description: Close-up: Jonathan’s face, now in the harsh light of a new morning. He looks unhinged, eyes wide with terror, sweat beading on his forehead. Heavy shadows under his eyes. He looks directly at the viewer as if pleading for sanity.

bubbles.text: God preserve my sanity! I may not go mad, if, indeed, I be not mad already.

panels.description: Medium shot: Jonathan clutching his tablets (diary) to his chest as if they are a shield. He looks over his shoulder toward the dark shadows of the room, thinking of the Count. The lighting is high-contrast, emphasizing his fear of the Count's hold over him.

bubbles.text: The Count's mysterious warning... it frightens me more now. I shall fear to doubt what he may say!

panels.description: Medium shot: Jonathan, looking weary but showing a flicker of rebellion, drags a large, heavy couch across the dusty floor. He is positioning it near the window, determined to sleep here rather than return to his usual rooms.

bubbles.text: I took a pleasure in disobeying him. I determined not to return to-night to the gloom-haunted rooms.

panels.description: Wide shot, high angle: Jonathan lies asleep on the couch by the window. The room is still and bathed in brilliant moonlight. On the floor, a clear trail of his footsteps is visible in the thick grey dust, leading from the door to the couch, marking his lonely presence in the void.

bubbles.text: I could see along the floor, in the brilliant moonlight, my own footsteps marked in the long accumulation of dust.
